questionHtml: HGH is a peptide hormone. Describe the mode of action of peptide hormones on target cells.
markschemeHtml:
a. peptide hormones do not enter the cell ✔

b. they bind to receptors/proteins in the plasma membrane of the target cell ✔

c. a secondary messenger initiates the cell response ✔

d. causes a cascade of actions that changes the cell’s physiology ✔

e. cAMP is a common secondary messenger ✔

meta.title: HGH is a peptide hormone. Describe the mode of action of peptide hormones on target cells.
breadcrumbLabels: D3.3.3. Regulation of blood glucose as an example of the role of hormones in homeostasis
